promptJapanese: 今日は天気がいいですね。
modelAnswers: The weather is nice today, isn't it?
It's a beautiful day today.

promptJapanese: お腹が空きました。
modelAnswers: I'm hungry.
I'm starving.

promptJapanese: この映画はとても面白かったです。
modelAnswers: This movie was very interesting.
I really enjoyed this movie.

promptJapanese: 昨日、友達と公園を散歩しました。
modelAnswers: I took a walk in the park with my friend yesterday.
Yesterday, I went for a walk in the park with a friend.

promptJapanese: もう少しゆっくり話してもらえますか？
modelAnswers: Could you speak a little more slowly, please?
Can you slow down a bit?

promptJapanese: 週末は何をする予定ですか？
modelAnswers: What are you going to do this weekend?
What are your plans for the weekend?

promptJapanese: 彼女はその知らせを聞いて、泣いてしまいました。
modelAnswers: She burst into tears when she heard the news.
She cried when she heard the news.

promptJapanese: 東京に住んで10年になります。
modelAnswers: I have been living in Tokyo for ten years.
It's been ten years since I moved to Tokyo.

promptJapanese: 会議は3時に始まります。
modelAnswers: The meeting starts at 3 o'clock.
The meeting is scheduled for 3 PM.

promptJapanese: 資料を送っていただけますか？
modelAnswers: Could you send me the documents?
Would you be able to send me the materials?

promptJapanese: 明日の午後なら空いています。
modelAnswers: I'm available tomorrow afternoon.
Tomorrow afternoon works for me.

promptJapanese: この件についてご検討いただけますか？
modelAnswers: Could you please consider this matter?
Would you mind looking into this issue?
grammarPoint: 丁寧な依頼・ビジネス表現

promptJapanese: 締め切りを来週に延ばしてもいいですか？
modelAnswers: Could we push the deadline back to next week?
Is it possible to extend the deadline to next week?

promptJapanese: ご不便をおかけして申し訳ありません。
modelAnswers: I'm sorry for the inconvenience.
We apologize for any inconvenience caused.

promptJapanese: そのプロジェクトは予算内で完了させる必要があります。
modelAnswers: We need to complete the project within budget.
The project must be finished within the budget.

promptJapanese: 先ほどのご説明をもう少し詳しくお聞かせいただけますか？
modelAnswers: Could you elaborate a bit more on what you just explained?
Would you mind giving us more details on your earlier explanation?

promptJapanese: 駅はどこですか？
modelAnswers: Where is the station?
Could you tell me where the station is?

promptJapanese: チェックインをお願いします。
modelAnswers: I'd like to check in, please.
I have a reservation. I'd like to check in.

promptJapanese: 空港までどのくらいかかりますか？
modelAnswers: How long does it take to get to the airport?
How far is it to the airport?

promptJapanese: この近くにレストランはありますか？
modelAnswers: Is there a restaurant near here?
Are there any restaurants around here?

promptJapanese: 窓側の席をお願いできますか？
modelAnswers: Could I have a window seat, please?
I'd prefer a window seat, if possible.

promptJapanese: 荷物が見当たりません。
modelAnswers: I can't find my luggage.
My baggage seems to be missing.

promptJapanese: この電車はセントラル駅に停まりますか？
modelAnswers: Does this train stop at Central Station?
Will this train stop at Central Station?

promptJapanese: 私はエンジニアとして働いています。
modelAnswers: I work as an engineer.
I'm an engineer.

promptJapanese: 趣味は写真を撮ることです。
modelAnswers: My hobby is taking photos.
I enjoy photography.

promptJapanese: 大学では経済学を専攻していました。
modelAnswers: I majored in economics at university.
My major was economics in college.
grammarPoint: 過去形・専攻の言い方

promptJapanese: 英語をもっと上達させたいと思っています。
modelAnswers: I want to improve my English.
I'm hoping to get better at English.

promptJapanese: 週に2回、ジムに通っています。
modelAnswers: I go to the gym twice a week.
I work out at the gym two times a week.

promptJapanese: 試験に合格してとても嬉しいです。
modelAnswers: I'm so happy that I passed the exam.
I'm thrilled to have passed the exam.

promptJapanese: プレゼンの前はいつも緊張します。
modelAnswers: I always get nervous before presentations.
I'm always nervous before giving a presentation.

promptJapanese: 彼の冗談には笑わずにはいられませんでした。
modelAnswers: I couldn't help laughing at his joke.
I couldn't stop laughing at his joke.

promptJapanese: その映画を見て感動しました。
modelAnswers: I was moved by the movie.
The movie really touched my heart.

promptJapanese: これはいくらですか？
modelAnswers: How much is this?
What's the price of this?

promptJapanese: もう少し安くなりますか？
modelAnswers: Could you give me a discount?
Is there any way to lower the price?

promptJapanese: 試着してもいいですか？
modelAnswers: May I try this on?
Can I try this on, please?

promptJapanese: 別の色はありますか？
modelAnswers: Do you have this in another color?
Are there other color options available?

promptJapanese: 頭が痛いです。
modelAnswers: I have a headache.
My head hurts.

promptJapanese: 熱が38度あります。
modelAnswers: I have a fever of 38 degrees.
My temperature is 38 degrees Celsius.

promptJapanese: この薬は1日3回飲んでください。
modelAnswers: Please take this medicine three times a day.
Take this medication three times daily.

promptJapanese: 最近、よく眠れていません。
modelAnswers: I haven't been sleeping well lately.
I've been having trouble sleeping recently.

promptJapanese: 会議は3時から始まります。
modelAnswers: The meeting starts at 3 o'clock.
The meeting begins at 3.
grammarPoint: 現在形で確定した予定を表す
scene: 社内スケジュール調整
explanation: 確定したスケジュールは will より現在形の方が自然。TOEICでは 'The meeting is scheduled for...' も頻出。
commonMistake: × 'will start' も正しいが、確定予定は現在形がより自然な英語。
exampleSentence: The conference call starts at 9 AM sharp.

promptJapanese: 議事録を確認していただけますか？
modelAnswers: Could you review the meeting minutes?
Would you please check the meeting minutes?
grammarPoint: Could you...? で丁寧な依頼
scene: 会議後のフォローアップ
explanation: 'minutes' は会議の文脈では「議事録」の意味。Could you / Would you の違い：どちらも丁寧だが Would you の方がやや格式高い。
commonMistake: × 'minute' と単数にしない。議事録は必ず 'minutes'（複数形）。
exampleSentence: Could you distribute the meeting minutes to all attendees?

promptJapanese: この件については後ほど議論しましょう。
modelAnswers: Let's discuss this matter later.
We'll address this issue later.
explanation: Let's は提案・勧誘を表す。'discuss' の後に 'about' は不要。
commonMistake: × 'Let's discuss about this' — discuss の後に about は不要。
exampleSentence: Let's discuss the budget details at the next meeting.

promptJapanese: 定例会議は毎週月曜日に行われます。
modelAnswers: The regular meeting is held every Monday.
The weekly meeting takes place every Monday.
grammarPoint: 受動態 is held / takes place
scene: 社内スケジュール案内
explanation: 'take place' は主語が人以外のイベントに使う表現。受動態 'is held' も同意。
commonMistake: × 'is happened' とは言わない。happen は自動詞なので受動態は不可。
exampleSentence: The quarterly review takes place every three months.

promptJapanese: ご出席いただけますか？
modelAnswers: Will you be able to attend?
Are you available to attend the meeting?
explanation: 'attend' は他動詞で目的語を直接取る。'attend to' は「対処する」と別の意味になるので注意。
commonMistake: × 'attend to the meeting' とは言わない（これは「会議に対処する」になる）。
exampleSentence: Will you be able to attend the annual conference?

promptJapanese: 次の議題に移りましょう。
modelAnswers: Let's move on to the next agenda item.
Shall we move on to the next item?
explanation: 'agenda' は会議の議題リスト全体、'agenda item' は個々の議題。'move on to' は「～に移る」という頻出表現。
commonMistake: × 'next agenda' だけでは不自然。'next agenda item' または 'next topic' と言う。
exampleSentence: Let's move on to the next agenda item regarding the budget.

promptJapanese: 本日の会議はオンラインで行います。
modelAnswers: Today's meeting will be held online.
The meeting today will take place online.
grammarPoint: will be held（受動態・未来）
explanation: オンライン会議の案内では 'virtually' や 'remotely' も使える。
commonMistake: × 'will held' は誤り。be動詞と過去分詞を忘れずに（will be held）。
exampleSentence: The board meeting will be held online via Zoom.

promptJapanese: 7時に2名で予約したいのですが。
modelAnswers: I'd like to make a reservation for two at 7 o'clock.
I'd like to book a table for two at 7 PM.
keyPhrase: make a reservation / book a table
explanation: 'reservation' はフォーマル、'booking' はやや口語的。for two は「2名で」。
commonMistake: × 'reserve for two persons' より 'for two' の方が自然。
exampleSentence: I'd like to make a reservation for four at 8 PM.

promptJapanese: 予約を変更できますか？
modelAnswers: Can I change my reservation?
Is it possible to modify my reservation?
explanation: 'modify' はよりフォーマルな表現。ホテルや航空券では 'amend' も使われる。
commonMistake: × 'change the reservation' でも通じるが 'my reservation' と所有格を使う方が自然。
exampleSentence: I'd like to modify my hotel reservation for next Tuesday.

promptJapanese: 予約を確認したいのですが。
modelAnswers: I'd like to confirm my reservation.
I'm calling to confirm my booking.
explanation: 電話で予約確認する際の典型表現。'I'm calling to...' は電話の目的を最初に伝える定型句。
commonMistake: × 'confirm about my reservation' — confirm の後に about は不要。
exampleSentence: I'm calling to confirm my reservation for this Friday.

promptJapanese: 予約をキャンセルしたいのですが。
modelAnswers: I'd like to cancel my reservation.
I need to cancel my booking.
explanation: キャンセル時は理由を添えると丁寧。'Unfortunately, I need to cancel...' という前置きも頻出。
commonMistake: × 'I want to cancel' は正しいが、フォーマルな場面では 'I'd like to' の方が適切。
exampleSentence: I'm afraid I need to cancel my reservation due to a scheduling conflict.

promptJapanese: 何名様でご利用ですか？
modelAnswers: How many people will be in your party?
How many guests will there be?
scene: ホテル・レストランのチェックイン
explanation: 'party' はここでは「パーティー」ではなく「グループ・一行」の意味。TOEICで頻出。
commonMistake: × 'How many persons?' より 'How many people?' または 'How many guests?' が自然。
exampleSentence: How many people will be in your party this evening?

promptJapanese: ご希望のお日にちはいつですか？
modelAnswers: What date do you prefer?
What is your preferred date?
keyPhrase: preferred date / What date do you prefer?
explanation: 'preferred' は「希望の・好みの」という意味の形容詞。フォーマルな接客英語で頻出。
commonMistake: × 'What date you prefer?' — 疑問文なので do が必要 (What date do you prefer?)。
exampleSentence: What is your preferred date for the appointment?

promptJapanese: 納期はいつですか？
modelAnswers: When is the delivery deadline?
What is the expected delivery date?
explanation: 'deadline' は締め切り、'delivery date' は配達予定日。TOEICのPart 7で頻出の語句。
commonMistake: × '納期' を直訳して '納 deadline' とは言わない。'delivery deadline' が自然。
exampleSentence: Could you confirm the delivery deadline for this order?

promptJapanese: 注文は来週末までに届きますか？
modelAnswers: Will the order arrive by the end of next week?
Can the order be delivered by next weekend?
scene: 納品スケジュール確認
explanation: 'by' は期限を表す前置詞（〜までに）。'until' は継続を表すので期限には by を使う。
commonMistake: × 'arrive until the end of next week' — 期限は by、継続は until。
exampleSentence: Will the shipment arrive by the end of this month?

promptJapanese: 納期を延ばしていただけますか？
modelAnswers: Could you extend the delivery deadline?
Is it possible to push back the delivery date?
explanation: 'push back' はインフォーマルだが実務でよく使う。'extend' はフォーマル。
commonMistake: × 'delay the deadline' は「締め切りを遅らせる」という意味で通じるが、自然な表現は 'extend'。
exampleSentence: Could we push back the deadline by one week?

promptJapanese: 商品は予定通りに発送されました。
modelAnswers: The goods were shipped as scheduled.
The items were dispatched on schedule.
grammarPoint: 受動態の過去形 were shipped
explanation: 'as scheduled' と 'on schedule' はほぼ同じ意味。shipped / dispatched / sent out どれも使える。
commonMistake: × 'The goods shipped' — 物が自分で発送することはないので受動態が必要。
exampleSentence: Your order was shipped as scheduled and will arrive by Friday.

promptJapanese: 遅延が発生する可能性があります。
modelAnswers: There may be a delay.
There is a possibility of a delay.
grammarPoint: There may be...（可能性の表現）
explanation: 'may' は可能性（～かもしれない）。'might' はやや低い可能性。両方頻出。
commonMistake: × 'There will be a delay' は確定表現なので、可能性がある場合は may/might を使う。
exampleSentence: Due to bad weather, there may be a delay in delivery.

promptJapanese: 送料は含まれていますか？
modelAnswers: Is shipping included in the price?
Does the price include shipping costs?
keyPhrase: shipping cost / included in the price
explanation: 'shipping' は「送料」、'handling' は「梱包手数料」。合わせて 'shipping and handling' とも言う。
commonMistake: × 'Is the shipping included?' も通じるが 'Is shipping included?' の方が自然。
exampleSentence: Is international shipping included in the total cost?

promptJapanese: コピー機が故障しています。
modelAnswers: The copier is out of order.
The printer is broken.
explanation: 'out of order' は機器の故障を表す定型表現。TOEIC Part 1の写真問題でも頻出。
commonMistake: × 'The copier is broken order' という言い方は存在しない。
exampleSentence: I'm sorry, but the elevator is out of order today.

promptJapanese: 会議室を予約してもらえますか？
modelAnswers: Could you book the conference room?
Could you reserve the meeting room for us?
explanation: 'conference room' と 'meeting room' は同じ意味。'book' はカジュアル、'reserve' はフォーマル。
commonMistake: × 'Can you reserve' でも通じるが、依頼には Could you の方が丁寧。
exampleSentence: Could you book the large conference room for 3 PM tomorrow?

promptJapanese: このフォームに記入してください。
modelAnswers: Please fill out this form.
Please complete this form.
keyPhrase: fill out a form（フォームに記入する）
explanation: 'fill out' と 'fill in' はほぼ同義。米英で使い方が若干異なる。
commonMistake: × 'fill in this form' も正しいが、米国では 'fill out' が一般的。
exampleSentence: Please fill out the registration form before the event.

promptJapanese: 書類を提出する期限はいつですか？
modelAnswers: What is the deadline for submitting the documents?
When is the document submission deadline?
explanation: 'submission' は 'submit'（提出する）の名詞形。deadline for + 動名詞の構造が重要。
commonMistake: × 'deadline to submit' も正しいが、'for submitting' の方がフォーマルな書き言葉では自然。
exampleSentence: What is the deadline for submitting the expense reports?

promptJapanese: 上司に確認します。
modelAnswers: I'll check with my supervisor.
Let me confirm with my manager.
scene: 社内コミュニケーション
explanation: 'check with someone' は「〜に確認する」という頻出表現。supervisor（直属の上司）とmanager（管理職）は似ているが使い分けに注意。
commonMistake: × 'I'll check to my boss' — with を使って 'check with my boss' が正しい。
exampleSentence: I'll need to check with my supervisor before I can confirm.

promptJapanese: 明日は在宅勤務の予定です。
modelAnswers: I'm planning to work from home tomorrow.
I'll be working remotely tomorrow.
explanation: 'WFH'（Work From Home）はビジネスの略語として頻用。'remotely' も同義。
commonMistake: × 'work at home' も意味は通じるが、'work from home' が標準的な表現。
exampleSentence: Our team will be working from home until further notice.

promptJapanese: 資料を印刷してもらえますか？
modelAnswers: Could you print the materials for me?
Would you mind printing out the handouts?
explanation: 'handout' は会議や授業で配る資料。'materials' はより広い意味での資料。
commonMistake: × 'Would you mind to print' — mind の後は動名詞（-ing形）。
exampleSentence: Would you mind printing 20 copies of the handouts?

promptJapanese: 空港まで何分かかりますか？
modelAnswers: How long does it take to get to the airport?
How many minutes does it take to the airport?
grammarPoint: How long does it take to...?
keyPhrase: How long does it take to...?
explanation: 所要時間を聞く最重要表現。It takes + 時間 + to do で「〜するのに〜かかる」。
commonMistake: × 'How long it takes?' — 疑問文なので does の倒置が必要。
exampleSentence: How long does it take to get to the downtown area from here?

promptJapanese: 次の電車は何時ですか？
modelAnswers: What time is the next train?
When is the next train?
explanation: 交通機関の時刻を聞く定型表現。'When does the next train depart?' も同義。
commonMistake: × 'What time the next train is?' — 疑問文は助動詞・be動詞を前に出す。
exampleSentence: Excuse me, what time is the next bus to the city center?

promptJapanese: タクシーを呼んでいただけますか？
modelAnswers: Could you call a taxi for me?
Could you hail a cab?
keyPhrase: call a taxi / hail a cab
explanation: 'hail' は手を上げてタクシーを止めること。米国では 'cab'、英国では 'taxi' が一般的。
commonMistake: × 'call taxi' — 冠詞 a が必要 (call a taxi)。
exampleSentence: Could you call a taxi for me? I need to get to the airport by 6.

promptJapanese: 乗り換えは必要ですか？
modelAnswers: Do I need to transfer?
Will I need to change trains?
explanation: 電車の乗り換えは 'transfer' または 'change trains'。バスは 'change buses'。
commonMistake: × 'Do I need to transit?' — transit は不可算名詞として使われることが多く、乗り換えには transfer/change が自然。
exampleSentence: Do I need to transfer to get to the airport?

promptJapanese: この電車は終点まで行きますか？
modelAnswers: Does this train go all the way to the last stop?
Does this train go to the final destination?
grammarPoint: go all the way to（〜まで行く）
explanation: 'all the way to' は強調表現で「ずっと〜まで」。乗り換えなしで行けるかを確認する際に使う。
commonMistake: × 'Does this train go until the last stop?' — 場所に until は不自然。to を使う。
exampleSentence: Does this bus go all the way to the central station?

promptJapanese: ご連絡いただきありがとうございます。
modelAnswers: Thank you for contacting us.
Thank you for reaching out.
grammarPoint: Thank you for -ing（〜してくれてありがとう）
scene: ビジネスメール書き出し
keyPhrase: Thank you for reaching out（連絡してくれてありがとう）
explanation: メールの冒頭定型句。'reaching out' はビジネスメールで非常に頻出。
commonMistake: × 'Thank you for your contact' — contact を名詞で使うと不自然。Thank you for contacting us が正しい。
exampleSentence: Thank you for reaching out to our customer service team.

promptJapanese: 添付ファイルをご確認ください。
modelAnswers: Please see the attached file.
Please find the attachment below.
grammarPoint: Please find / Please see（確認してください）
explanation: 'Please find attached...' は最もフォーマルな表現。TOEICのPart 7に頻出。
commonMistake: × 'Please check the attachment file' — 'attached file' または 'attachment'（attachment fileとは言わない）。
exampleSentence: Please find the report attached to this email.

promptJapanese: ご返信いただければ幸いです。
modelAnswers: I would appreciate your reply.
I look forward to hearing from you.
grammarPoint: I look forward to -ing（〜を楽しみにしています）
keyPhrase: I look forward to hearing from you.
explanation: メールの結びの定型句。'I'm looking forward to...' は口語、'I look forward to...' はより格式高い書き言葉。
commonMistake: × 'I look forward to hear from you' — to の後は動名詞（hearing）。
exampleSentence: I look forward to hearing from you at your earliest convenience.

promptJapanese: 詳細については後日ご連絡いたします。
modelAnswers: I will get back to you with the details.
I will follow up with more information.
grammarPoint: get back to you（後で連絡する）
keyPhrase: get back to you / follow up
explanation: 'get back to you' はビジネス英語で最頻出の表現のひとつ。'follow up' は追ってフォローする意。
commonMistake: × 'I will contact to you' — contact は他動詞なので to は不要。
exampleSentence: I'll get back to you with the pricing details by end of day.

promptJapanese: ご不明な点がございましたらお知らせください。
modelAnswers: Please let me know if you have any questions.
Feel free to contact me if you need any clarification.
keyPhrase: Please let me know if you have any questions.
explanation: メールの締め括りに最もよく使われる表現。'clarification' は「説明・明確化」。
commonMistake: × 'Please tell me if you have questions' でも通じるが、フォーマルなメールでは 'let me know' が自然。
exampleSentence: Please don't hesitate to contact me if you have any questions.

promptJapanese: 以下の通りご確認をお願いします。
modelAnswers: Please confirm the following.
Kindly review the information below.
explanation: 'Kindly' は Please よりやや格式高い。アジア系ビジネス英語で特によく使われる。
commonMistake: × 'Please check below' でも意味は通じるが、'Please confirm the following' がよりフォーマル。
exampleSentence: Kindly review the contract and confirm your acceptance.

promptJapanese: 応募書類を送っていただけますか？
modelAnswers: Could you send us your application documents?
Please submit your application materials.
explanation: 米国では 'resume'（1〜2ページの履歴書）、英国では 'CV'（より詳細）が一般的。
commonMistake: × 'entry sheet' は和製英語。英語では 'application form' または 'job application'。
exampleSentence: Please send us your resume and cover letter by the end of the week.

promptJapanese: 面接の日程を調整したいのですが。
modelAnswers: I'd like to schedule an interview.
I'm writing to arrange an interview time.
explanation: 'schedule' と 'arrange' はどちらも「手配・予定する」の意。メールでは 'I'm writing to...' で書き出すのが定石。
commonMistake: × 'I want to decide the interview date' — schedule/arrange が自然な表現。
exampleSentence: I'd like to schedule a phone interview at your earliest convenience.

promptJapanese: 貴社に応募したいと思っています。
modelAnswers: I would like to apply for a position at your company.
I am interested in applying for the open position.
grammarPoint: apply for a position（職に応募する）
keyPhrase: apply for a position / open position
explanation: 'open position' は「空きポジション」。'vacancy' も同義のフォーマル語。
commonMistake: × 'I want to apply to your company' — 職への応募は apply for（職）at（会社）。
exampleSentence: I would like to apply for the marketing manager position advertised on your website.

promptJapanese: この職種の経験はどのくらいありますか？
modelAnswers: How much experience do you have in this field?
How long have you been working in this industry?
grammarPoint: How much experience...? / How long have you...?
keyPhrase: experience in this field（この分野の経験）
explanation: 面接の定番質問。'field' は「分野」、'industry' は「業界」とやや異なる。
commonMistake: × 'How many experience' — experience は不可算名詞なので How much を使う。
exampleSentence: How much experience do you have in project management?

promptJapanese: 採用が決まりましたらご連絡します。
modelAnswers: We will contact you if you are selected.
You will hear from us if your application is successful.
grammarPoint: if 節（条件）+ 受動態
keyPhrase: if you are selected / if successful
explanation: 'You will hear from us' は「こちらからご連絡します」の意。採用連絡の定型表現。
commonMistake: × 'We will contact to you' — contact は他動詞で to は不要。
exampleSentence: Successful candidates will be contacted within two weeks.

promptJapanese: 給与の詳細を教えていただけますか？
modelAnswers: Could you tell me about the salary details?
Could you provide information on the compensation package?
grammarPoint: compensation package（給与・待遇パッケージ）
explanation: 'compensation package' は給与・ボーナス・福利厚生を含む待遇全体。フォーマルな表現。
commonMistake: × 'treatment' を「待遇」と直訳しない。英語では 'benefits' または 'compensation'。
exampleSentence: Could you tell me more about the compensation package and benefits?

promptJapanese: 請求書を送っていただけますか？
modelAnswers: Could you send me the invoice?
Please provide an invoice for the services.
explanation: 'invoice' は商取引の請求書。'bill' は一般的な勘定書き（レストランなど）。両方TOEICで頻出。
commonMistake: × 'send me the bill' も通じるが、ビジネス取引では 'invoice' が正式。
exampleSentence: Please email the invoice to our accounts payable department.

promptJapanese: 支払い期限はいつですか？
modelAnswers: When is the payment due?
What is the due date for payment?
grammarPoint: due date / payment due（支払い期限）
explanation: 'due' は「期限の」という形容詞。'due date' はTOEICのメールや文書で最頻出語の一つ。
commonMistake: × 'When is the deadline of payment?' — due date の方が自然で頻出。
exampleSentence: The payment is due within 30 days of the invoice date.

promptJapanese: この金額は正しいですか？
modelAnswers: Is this amount correct?
Could you verify this amount?
explanation: 'verify' は「正確さを確認する」フォーマル語。'double-check' は「念のため再確認する」。
commonMistake: × 'Is this price right?' でも通じるが、請求書文脈では 'amount' と 'correct' が適切。
exampleSentence: Could you verify the total amount on this invoice?

promptJapanese: 請求書番号を教えていただけますか？
modelAnswers: Could you tell me the invoice number?
What is the invoice number for this order?
explanation: 問い合わせ時に必要な参照番号。'reference number' も同様によく使われる。
commonMistake: × 'invoice no.' は略語として書類上では使うが、口頭・メールでは 'invoice number' が自然。
exampleSentence: Please quote the invoice number in all correspondence.

promptJapanese: 銀行振込でお支払いできますか？
modelAnswers: Can I pay by bank transfer?
Is bank transfer an accepted payment method?
explanation: 'bank transfer' と 'wire transfer' はほぼ同義。米国では 'wire transfer' が一般的。
commonMistake: × 'pay with bank transfer' — 支払い手段には pay by を使う。
exampleSentence: We accept payment by bank transfer, credit card, or check.

promptJapanese: 領収書を発行していただけますか？
modelAnswers: Could you issue a receipt?
Could you provide a receipt for this payment?
grammarPoint: issue a receipt（領収書を発行する）
explanation: 'receipt' は支払い証明書。'invoice' は請求書、'receipt' は領収書と区別する。
commonMistake: × 'receipt' と 'recipe' を混同しない。receipt（領収書）はレシートのこと。
exampleSentence: Could you issue an itemized receipt for my expense report?

promptJapanese: この商品の保証期間はどのくらいですか？
modelAnswers: What is the warranty period for this product?
How long is the warranty on this item?
explanation: 'warranty' は製品保証、'guarantee' はより広い保証。TOEICのPart 7でよく登場する。
commonMistake: × 'guarantee period' も通じるが、製品の保証には 'warranty' が業界標準語。
exampleSentence: The warranty period for this laptop is two years from the date of purchase.

promptJapanese: 在庫はありますか？
modelAnswers: Is this item in stock?
Do you have this in stock?
explanation: 'out of stock' は在庫切れ。'back-ordered' は「入荷待ち」。全てTOEICで頻出。
commonMistake: × 'Do you have stock?' — 「在庫確認」は in stock の表現が自然。
exampleSentence: I'm afraid this model is currently out of stock.

promptJapanese: 返品は可能ですか？
modelAnswers: Is it possible to return this item?
Can I return this product?
grammarPoint: Is it possible to...? / return policy
explanation: 'return policy' は返品規定。'refund' は返金、'exchange' は交換。
commonMistake: × 'Can I send back this item?' でも通じるが、'return' が標準的な表現。
exampleSentence: What is your return policy for online purchases?

promptJapanese: 別のサイズはありますか？
modelAnswers: Do you have this in a different size?
Is this available in other sizes?
scene: ショッピング・商品選択
explanation: 'Do you have this in + 色/サイズ' は買い物の定番表現。'available in' も同様に使える。
commonMistake: × 'Do you have other size?' — サイズ・色などはこの構文で a different / other を使う。
exampleSentence: Do you have this jacket available in a larger size?

promptJapanese: この商品の主な特徴を教えていただけますか？
modelAnswers: Could you tell me about the main features of this product?
What are the key features of this item?
explanation: 'feature' はデジタル製品や機器の「機能・特徴」。'specification' は詳細なスペック。
commonMistake: × 'characteristics' も使えるが、製品の機能説明では 'features' が最も一般的。
exampleSentence: Let me walk you through the key features of our new software.

promptJapanese: 割引はありますか？
modelAnswers: Is there any discount available?
Do you offer any discounts?
explanation: 'bulk discount' は大量購入割引。'seasonal discount' は季節割引。TOEICの広告問題で頻出。
commonMistake: × 'Is there discount?' — 不可算名詞的用法でも any を使って 'any discount' の方が自然。
exampleSentence: Do you offer any discounts for bulk orders?

promptJapanese: 本日は休業日となっております。
modelAnswers: We are closed today.
Our office is closed today.
keyPhrase: closed for the day / business hours
explanation: 'closed' は閉店・休業状態。'We are not available today.' も同義。TOEIC Part 2の短文問題で頻出。
commonMistake: × 'We are holiday today' — closed を使って 'We are closed for the holiday' が正しい。
exampleSentence: Please note that we will be closed on all national holidays.

promptJapanese: 営業時間は9時から18時までです。
modelAnswers: Our business hours are from 9 AM to 6 PM.
We are open from 9 in the morning until 6 in the evening.
explanation: 'business hours' は会社・店舗の営業時間。英国では 'opening hours' が一般的。
commonMistake: × 'service time' は和製英語的。'business hours' または 'operating hours' が正しい。
exampleSentence: Our customer service is available during regular business hours.

promptJapanese: システムメンテナンスのためサービスを一時停止します。
modelAnswers: We will temporarily suspend our service for system maintenance.
Our service will be unavailable due to scheduled maintenance.
keyPhrase: scheduled maintenance（定期メンテナンス）
explanation: 'due to' は理由を表す前置詞句。'because of' と同義だが書き言葉・フォーマルな文でよく使われる。
commonMistake: × 'due to we are doing maintenance' — due to の後は名詞句。
exampleSentence: The website will be unavailable from 2 to 4 AM due to scheduled maintenance.

promptJapanese: 新しいサービスを開始いたします。
modelAnswers: We are launching a new service.
We are pleased to announce the launch of our new service.
grammarPoint: launch（立ち上げる）/ We are pleased to announce
keyPhrase: launch（発表・開始）/ We are pleased to announce
explanation: 'We are pleased to announce' はフォーマルな告知の定型句。'launch' は製品・サービスの開始に使う。
commonMistake: × 'We will start new service' — launch を使い 'a new service' と冠詞を忘れない。
exampleSentence: We are pleased to announce the launch of our premium membership plan.

promptJapanese: 詳細はウェブサイトをご確認ください。
modelAnswers: Please visit our website for more details.
For further information, please refer to our website.
grammarPoint: for more details / for further information
explanation: 案内文の締め括りに使う定型表現。'refer to' は「〜を参照する」。
commonMistake: × 'Please see our website' でも通じるが、'visit our website' または 'refer to our website' が自然。
exampleSentence: For more details on the event, please visit our official website.

promptJapanese: ご不便をおかけして誠に申し訳ございません。
modelAnswers: We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience.
We are sorry for any inconvenience this may cause.
explanation: 'inconvenience' は「不便・迷惑」。'We apologize for...' はビジネス文書の謝罪定型句。
commonMistake: × 'We are very sorry for inconvenience' — any をつけて 'any inconvenience' が自然。
exampleSentence: We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience caused by this service interruption.
